advisory.text: infoRide app pick-up locations for Terminal 5 and 7 have temporarily relocated to the Howard Beach AirTrain Station due to construction at JFK. Find Terminal 5 and Terminal 7 Information.

h1: Uber, Lyft and Other Ride Share Apps
lede: The AirTrain connects passengers to terminals, airport parking lots, the hotel shuttle area, the rental car center, and public transportation.Learn about the JFK AirTrain

blocks.h2: Getting to and from JFK Airport via ride apps
paragraphs: Uber and Lyft are popular ride share apps available to get to and from JFK for your convenience. Follow the signs after baggage claim to find pickup areas prior to requesting a ride.

To book a ride, download one of the apps and follow instructions to find your pick up location. Always be sure to check the car’s license plate against the information in the app to ensure you are in the right vehicle.

blocks.h2: Ride app pick-up and drop-off locations
h3s: Ride app costs to and from JFK
paragraphs: Passengers can generally be picked up and dropped off in front of their terminals, but this may vary depending on the time of day and terminal. Check your app for the latest details on pick-up and drop-off locations.
Costs to and from the airport vary depending on your location, as well as the time of day. Check your app for exact pricing details.
Uber and Lyft provide accessible vehicle options, including wheelchair ramps and extra space, available for pickup in front of the terminal. Check your app for details.
For assistance from your gate to your ride, contact your airline prior to your flight to arrange wheelchair services.
Uber Shuttle is a shared shuttle service offered as a cheaper alternative and makes traveling between NYC and JFK Airport easy.
There are two routes servicing JFK between Port Authority (42nd St) and Greenwich Village, and you can reserve up to five seats in advance right in the Uber app.

links.text: Learn more about Uber Shuttle routes
